inlineCode: // LAVA RING + INNER BORDER + LAND FILL
// Thickness of outer land spawn band
y: centerY + r * tileHeight + q * yStep
// mark outer land rim as spawn points

events.comment: Second begin of scene load after tiles generated

inlineCode: // LAVA HEAT PULSE - STRONGER TEST VERSION
// Put in a normal JavaScript event that runs every frame
const speed = 2.4;     // faster so motion is obvious
const phase = t * speed + q * qOffset + r * rOffset;
const rCol = Math.max(0, Math.min(255, Math.round(baseR + strength * pulse)));
const gCol = Math.max(0, Math.min(255, Math.round(baseG + strength * 0.7 * pulse2)));
const bCol = Math.max(0, Math.min(255, Math.round(baseB + strength * 0.15 * pulse)));

parameters: "PRESS G to spawn a competitor at start point"+ NewLine()+NewLine()+"Press C to see centre point for initial resource drop"

events.comment: For now deletes but this shows we can randomly place the competitors

eventsFunctionsExtensions.author: D8H
eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: Sticker
eventsFunctionsExtensions.shortDescription: Stick objects to others, following position and rotation. For accessories/skeletons.
description: This extension can be useful to:
- Stick accessories to moving objects
- Animate a skeleton
- Delete an object with another one
An example allows to check it out ([open the project online](https://editor.gdevelop.io/?project=example://stick-objects)).

eventsFunctions.description: Define helper classes JavaScript code.
eventsFunctions.name: DefineHelperClasses

inlineCode: * Update the coordinates in the basisObject basis.
* It uses the basisObject coordinates from the previous frame.
* This way, the sticker can move relatively to it and still
// Save initial values to avoid calculus and rounding errors
* Copy the coordinates to use it the next frame.
* This method is also called by children to ensure
// If the behavior on the basis object has a different name,
// the objects will still follow their basis objects
// Follow parents 1st to avoid frame delays
// Unproportional dimensions changes won't work as expected
// if the object angle is not null but nothing more can be done
// because there is no full affine transformation on objects.

eventsFunctions.description: Check if the object is stuck to another object.
eventsFunctions.name: IsStuck

eventsBasedBehaviors.description: Stick the object to another. Use the action to stick the object, or unstick it later.

// Register this object as a sticker.

eventsFunctions.description: Stick on another object.
eventsFunctions.name: Stick

inlineCode: // An object can stick to only one object.

eventsFunctions.description: Unstick from the object it was stuck to.
eventsFunctions.name: Unstick

propertyDescriptors.label: Destroy when the object it's stuck on is destroyed
propertyDescriptors.name: IsDestroyedWithParent

eventsFunctionsExtensions.author: Arthur Pacaud (arthuro555)
eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: ArrayTools
eventsFunctionsExtensions.shortDescription: Array utilities: search, sort, shuffle, slice, concat, reverse, pop, random element access.
description: This extension adds features like:
- Combining arrays (concatenate into new array, append all children to another array)
- Finding a value in an array (find index expression, contains condition)
- Reversing arrays
- Get a portion of an array (slice)
- Add and remove lots of children at specific indices at once (fill, splice, shift, pop, add at index)
- Randomness (shuffle children, get a random child)
- Mathematical operations using arrays (sum of all numbers, biggest/smallest number, average number, median number)
- Sort numbers in an array
- String inter-operation (split string into array, join array array children to string)

inlineCode: // Ensure the target is a clean new array
// The `|| undefined` replaces 0 with undefined, as having the argument as 0
// would make the array empty and is therefore useless.
// With undefined though, it doesn't stop until the end of the array,
// Copy each variable of the slice into the new array

parameters.description: The index to end the slice at
parameters.longDescription:
Set to 0 to copy all of the array. If you use a negative value, the index will be selected beginning from the end.  
For example, slicing an array with 5 elements from 0 to -1 would take only elements from indices 0 to 3.
parameters.name: End

inlineCode: // Cast to array to ensure getAllChildrenArray returns the real array
// At the position of the array (i), remove 1 element (the array),
// and insert elements of b (the contents of the array) in-place of the array.
// Skip over the elements that were just added as we are flattening only 1 level deep
// If deep, the current element has been replaced with another, we need to recheck it on next iteration.
// If not deep, the current element is now the one after the array elements and needs to be checked too.
// In both case, decreasing by 1 is necessary.

parameters.longDescription: If yes, will continue flattening until there is no arrays in the array anymore.

eventsFunctions.description: Replaces all arrays inside of an array with their children. For example, [[1,2], [3,4]] becomes [1,2,3,4].
eventsFunctions.name: ObjectFlatten

inlineCode: // Cast to array to ensure getAllChildrenArray returns the real array
// At the position of the array (i), remove 1 element (the array),
// and insert elements of b (the contents of the array) in-place of the array.
// Skip over the elements that were just added as we are flattening only 1 level deep
// If deep, the current element has been replaced with another, we need to recheck it on next iteration.
// If not deep, the current element is now the one after the array elements and needs to be checked too.
// In both case, decreasing by 1 is necessary.

parameters.description: The object the variable is from
parameters.name: Object

parameters.longDescription: If yes, will continue flattening until there is no arrays in the array anymore.
parameters.name: Deep

eventsFunctionsExtensions.author: Tristan Rhodes (tristan@victrisgames.com)
eventsFunctionsExtensions.fullName: Make object travel to random positions
eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: TravelToRandomPositions
eventsFunctionsExtensions.shortDescription: Move objects to random nearby positions using Pathfinding. Optional direction bias.
description: When the action is used, the object(s) will select a random nearby 2D location and begin moving towards it (using the 2D Pathfinding behavior).

If desired, choose a direction and how closely you want the object to follow it.  This can be used to make objects chase (or run away from) the player, or to make objects cross the screen using a meandering path.
If you keep using the action, once the object reaches a destination, it will select a new random location and begin moving towards it.
If you want to modify the speed of the object, edit the Pathfinding behavior of the object.

eventsFunctions.description: Make object travel to a random position around the object current position. The movement is initiated only when the object is not moving already (its Pathfinding behavior speed is 0).  Move towards a specified angle, if desired.
eventsFunctions.fullName: Make object travel to a random position, with optional direction
eventsFunctions.name: TravelToRandomPositions
eventsFunctions.sentence: Move _PARAM1_ to random positions, where each stop is at least _PARAM3_px and at most _PARAM4_px from the current position. Move towards angle _PARAM5_ with a bias of _PARAM6_

events.comment: Create a new destination every time the object stops

parameters.description: Object that will be travelling (must have Pathfinding behavior)
parameters.name: Object

parameters.description: Direction (in degrees) the object will move towards (Range: 0-360)
parameters.name: Direction

parameters.longDescription: For example: "0" picks a completely random direction, "0.5" will select a direction within the half-circle that faces the specified direction, and "1" simply uses the specified direction.
parameters.name: DirectionBias

eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: ButtonStates
eventsFunctionsExtensions.shortDescription: Use any object as a button and change appearance according to user interactions.
description: Use the "Button states" behavior to track user interactions with an object, including:
- Hovered
- Pressed
- Clicked
- Idle
Add additional behaviors to make juicy buttons with animated responses to user input:
- Size
- Color
- Animation
- Object effects

events.comment: The "Validated" state only last one frame.
Make sure the cursor position is only checked once per frame.
Touches are always pressed, so ShouldCheckHovering doesn't matter.
